problem.title: Valid Anagram
problem.description: Given two strings s and t, return true if t is an anagram of s, and false otherwise.
assumptions: Same length strings can still fail if frequencies differ.

example.explanation: Both have same character frequencies. Sort both strings or use frequency map.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Character Count [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Increment a frequency counter for each character in s and decrement it for each character in t; if any count drops below zero a character appears more times in t than in s, so return false immediately. If all counts end at zero the strings are anagrams. The key insight is that one pass with a single fixed-size array (26 entries for lowercase letters) avoids sorting and achieves O(n) time with O(1) space.
